data.title: Implement isort-adapter.sh (isort CLI wrapper conforming to recipe-engine-adapter.md)
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Implement plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/isort-adapter.sh — bash adapter for Python import normalization using isort, conforming to recipe-engine-adapter.md.

### Implementation:
1. Engine validation: Check isort via `python3 -m isort --version` or `isort --version`. Parse version. Compare vs ISORT_MIN_VERSION (default "5.0.0"). On failure: degraded JSON {degraded:true, engine_name:"isort", exit_code:2}.
2. Recipe dispatch: Based on RECIPE_NAME=normalize-imports, run isort on RECIPE_PARAM_FILE or RECIPE_PARAM_DIR. Use --profile=black or --profile=pep8 based on RECIPE_PARAM_PROFILE (default: none, use isort defaults). NEVER shell-interpolate parameter values — use env var passthrough.
3. isort invocation: `python3 -m isort -- "$RECIPE_PARAM_FILE"` (using variable reference with `--` separator to prevent path injection) or `isort -- "$RECIPE_PARAM_FILE"`.
4. File change detection: Compare file content before/after using diff or hash. Populate files_changed array.
5. Git stash protocol: `git stash push --include-untracked -m "recipe-executor-isort-pre-run"` before; pop on success; pop on failure (handle empty stash gracefully).
6. Timeout: RECIPE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS env var (default 600). Use timeout command.
7. JSON output to stdout: {files_changed: [...], transforms_applied: N, errors: [], exit_code: 0, degraded: false, engine_name: "isort"}

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/isort-adapter.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/isort-adapter.sh
- [ ] Valid bash syntax
  Verify: bash -n plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/isort-adapter.sh
- [ ] All tests in test-isort-adapter.sh pass
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-isort-adapter.sh
- [ ] Missing engine returns exit_code=2
  Verify: PATH=/nonexistent bash plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/isort-adapter.sh 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); assert d.get('exit_code')==2"
